trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: "My lance was once wielded by Venerable Tarrian. In his name and by his might, I cast you down!" [Scryfall]. Venerable Tarrian is a historical figure in Ixalan's lore — a traveler who accompanied Saint Elenda and wrote the foundational texts later suppressed by the Church of Dusk on Ixalan. He was declared a heretic by the church for his writings on the vampire god Aclazotz, but venerated as a holy martyr by radical sects. His lance passed to Vito Quijano de Pasamonte, a vampire cleric who carried both the lance and Tarrian's journal. The unnamed knight in the card art is invoking Tarrian as a sacred forerunner. [Scryfall; MTG Wiki 'List of secondary characters/Ixalan', via WebSearch]
sources: MTG Wiki List of secondary characters/Ixalan, via WebSearch

trivia_bullets.text: Legion's Judgment was first printed in Ixalan (September 2017) — the Mesoamerican-inspired plane where the Sun Empire's dinosaur-riding knights and the Church of Dusk's vampires clashed. The card's mechanic (destroy creature with power 4 or greater) fits the Sun Empire's combat doctrine of cutting down the largest threats. It was reprinted in Core Set 2021 as a common white removal piece. [Scryfall prints search]

Legion's Judgment destroys any creature with power 4 or greater for three mana (2W) at sorcery speed. It answers large threats cleanly but misses small creatures entirely — a deliberate limited-format design that rewards opponents for going wide with small creatures. EDHREC rank of 21,706 places it near the bottom of Commander adoption; in Commander, unconditional removal (Swords to Plowshares, Path to Exile) is preferred. Its value is concentrated in limited and Pauper formats where the power-4 floor is a real constraint. [Scryfall]

Art by Lucas Graciano — depicting a golden-armored knight on a rearing white pegasus charging with a couched lance toward a massive purple dragon-beast. Graciano is a prolific MTG artist with a strong portfolio of figurative heroic scenes. The composition follows a classical chivalric-charge convention, no terrain grounding the duel — knight and monster hanging in open sky — which emphasizes the elevated, almost mythic register of the Tarrian invocation in the flavor text. [Scryfall]
